title: The loss of codiversified gut microbes predicts Autism Spectrum Disorder status and severity
authors_full: Emerson Taminang¹˒², Khemlal Nirmalkar¹, Rosa Krajmalnik-Brown¹, Taichi A. Suzuki¹˒²
authors_short: Emerson Taminang Khemlal Nirmalkar Rosa Krajmalnik-Brown Taichi A. Suzuki

summary.hook: This poster analyzes fecal shotgun metagenomic data from adults with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and typically developing (TD) controls collected before and after a microbiota...
summary.summary_30s: This poster analyzes fecal shotgun metagenomic data from adults with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and typically developing (TD) controls collected before and after a microbiota transplant therapy (MTT) trial.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster analyzes fecal shotgun metagenomic data from adults with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and typically developing (TD) controls collected before and after a...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Secondary analysis of fecal shotgun metagenomic data from a Phase 2 MTT trial (trial described as double-blind, placebo-controlled).. ASD subjects n=51; TD n=46
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Adults with ASD have a lower total relative abundance of codiversified microbes compared to TD controls (Wilcoxon rank sum test).
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: Loss of codiversified microbes may help distinguish ASD vs TD status and relate to severity, suggesting a biomarker direction for ASD-associated gut microbiome signals.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: How exactly are “codiversified” microbes defined in Suzuki et al. 2022, and what criteria produced the list of 36 species?

key_numbers: Two timepoints: (Time point 1) before MTT / no intervention; (Time point 2) after MTT. Exact calendar timing not stated...

takeaways: Adults with ASD have a lower total relative abundance of codiversified microbes compared to TD controls (Wilcoxon rank sum test).
A decrease in ASD severity after MTT is associated with an increase in total relative abundance of codiversified microbes (Spearman rho; shown at Time point 1 vs Time...
The total relative abundance of non-codiversified microbes does not predict ASD severity (Spearman rho; shown at Time point 1 and Time point 2).
